elements.title: Cruz de Clavos
elements.contents: ... that the original Cruz de Clavos (replica pictured), which included 260 nails representing women killed in Chihuahua, was later stolen despite being anchored to the ground?

elements.title: People in Trouble
elements.contents: ... that People in Trouble was based on Sarah Schulman's experiences of the AIDS crisis, Donald Trump, and having an affair with a married woman?

elements.title: Count Henry II of Champagne
elements.contents: ... that medieval sources disagree on exactly how Count Henry II of Champagne died in a fall, variously blaming a broken window bar, a carpet, or even a falling dwarf?

elements.title: Baptistery of Bekalta
elements.contents: ... that one archaeologist suggested that the shape of the Baptistery of Bekalta facilitated multiple baptisms at the same time?

elements.title: Jessie Catherine Couvreur
elements.contents: ... that Jessie Catherine Couvreur became so famous for her public lectures about Australia that she was invited to a private meeting with King Leopold II of Belgium?

elements.title: Sokoban
elements.contents: ... that the first Sokoban video game featured text-based graphics and only five levels, until a salesman saw it and suggested that it had commercial potential?

elements.title: Sophie Tea
elements.contents: ... that Sophie Tea once paid for accommodation by painting a cow?

elements.title: Docip
elements.contents: ... that Docip provided a computer room for indigenous peoples' representatives in international negotiations?

elements.title: Triyono Wibowo
elements.contents: ... that Indonesian diplomat Triyono Wibowo attempted to bring Komodo dragons and orangutans to Slovenia?
